title: Getting started with typethis
text: lowest-level entry points test, assert, validate types. Use function boundaries, parsing inputs, anywhere quick sanity check enough. is_type() accepts : builtin name — "numeric", "integer", "character", "logical", "list", "data.frame", "matrix", "factor", "date", "posixct", "function", "environment"; predicate function function(value) -> logical; type spec t_union("integer", "character"). safe coercion, use coerce_type():
code: is_type(5, "numeric") #> [1] TRUE is_type("hello", "numeric") #> [1] FALSE  # assert_type() throws on mismatch assert_type(5, "numeric", "x")  # validate_type() returns a list instead of throwing validate_type("hello", "numeric", "x") #> $valid #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $error #> [1] "Type error: 'x' must be numeric, got character" coerce_type("123", "numeric") #> [1] 123 coerce_type(c(1, 0, 1), "logical") #> [1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE

title: Getting started with typethis
text: typed_function() wraps function call validated. bad call fails body runs: typed_function() supports every R calling convention — positional, named, mixed, reordered, ... passthrough — respects argument defaults. Set coerce = TRUE convert mismatches checking, validate = FALSE hot paths ’ve already validated upstream. full feature set see ?typed_function validators--models vignette.
code: greet <- typed_function(   function(name, times = 1L) paste(rep(name, times), collapse = " "),   arg_specs   = list(name = "character", times = "integer"),   return_spec = "character" )  greet("hi", times = 3L) #> [1] "hi hi hi" greet("hi", times = "three") #> Error: #> ! Type error: 'times' must be integer, got character

title: Getting started with typethis
text: Use define_model() describe record type — configuration object, API request body, domain entity. generates new_<Class>() constructor update_<Class>() updater calling environment. Validation runs construction time: update_User() returns fresh instance named fields replaced revalidated: Models nest. Reference one model another class name: defaults, nullability, strict mode, validators, see validators--models vignette.
code: define_model("User", fields = list(   name  = field("character"),   age   = field("integer", validator = numeric_range(0, 120),                 default = 0L),   email = field("character",                 validator = string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$")) ))  ada <- new_User(name = "Ada Lovelace", email = "ada@example.com") ada #> <Typed Model: User> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada Lovelace #>   email: character = ada@example.com #>   age: integer = 0 new_User(name = "Ada", age = 200L, email = "ada@example.com") #> Error: #> ! Validation failed for field 'age' in User ada <- update_User(ada, age = 36L) ada$age #> [1] 36 define_model("Address", fields = list(   street = field("character"),   city   = field("character") ))  define_model("Person", fields = list(   name    = field("character"),   address = field("Address") ))  p <- new_Person(   name    = "Ada",   address = new_Address(street = "Newstead Abbey", city = "Nottingham") ) p$address$city #> [1] "Nottingham"

title: Getting started with typethis
text: t_*() family builds richer specifications work everywhere plain type name (is_type(), assert_type(), field(), typed_function(), to_json_schema()). Specs compose: Use spec inside field() typed_function()’s arg_specs entry. See type-specs vignette full reference.
code: id <- t_union("integer", "character") is_type(1L, id) #> [1] TRUE is_type("u-42", id) #> [1] TRUE  maybe_int <- t_nullable("integer") is_type(NULL, maybe_int) #> [1] TRUE  tags <- t_list_of("character", min_length = 1L) is_type(list("alpha", "beta"), tags) #> [1] TRUE  role <- t_enum(c("admin", "user", "guest")) is_type("admin", role) #> [1] TRUE mixed <- t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) is_type(list(1L, "two", 3L), mixed) #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting started with typethis
text: validators--models — built-validators, defaults, nullability, strict mode, field() metadata. type-specs — every t_*() constructor compose. interop — JSON Schema, Open Data Contract Standard, OpenAPI 3.1 export import. function reference, ?typethis topic-grouped index function’s help page “See also” section linking family.

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: to_json_schema() turns typed models, type specs, validators, [field()] definitions Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema fragment plain R list. Key behaviours: Built-validator constraints (numeric_range, string_length, string_pattern, vector_length, enum_validator) become native JSON Schema keywords (minimum, maxLength, pattern, minItems, enum). Nested models become $ref entries $defs. Cyclic references handled via stub--fill protocol. Constructs without canonical mapping (data frames, factors, custom predicate functions) emit x-typethis-* extension keys round-trip typethis-aware tooling. accepted inputs include type specs (to_json_schema(t_union(...))), validator closures (to_json_schema(numeric_range(0, 10))), field() definitions.
code: define_model("Person", fields = list(   name = field("character", nullable = FALSE),   age  = field("integer", validator = numeric_range(0, 120)),   role = field(t_enum(c("admin", "user")), default = "user") ))  schema <- to_json_schema("Person") str(schema, max.level = 2) #> List of 6 #>  $ $schema             : chr "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema" #>  $ title               : chr "Person" #>  $ type                : chr "object" #>  $ properties          :List of 3 #>   ..$ name:List of 1 #>   ..$ age :List of 3 #>   ..$ role:List of 3 #>  $ additionalProperties: logi TRUE #>  $ required            :List of 2 #>   ..$ : chr "name" #>   ..$ : chr "age" cat(jsonlite::toJSON(schema, auto_unbox = TRUE, pretty = TRUE)) #> { #>   "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema", #>   "title": "Person", #>   "type": "object", #>   "properties": { #>     "name": { #>       "type": "string" #>     }, #>     "age": { #>       "type": "integer", #>       "minimum": 0, #>       "maximum": 120 #>     }, #>     "role": { #>       "type": "string", #>       "enum": [ #>         "admin", #>         "user" #>       ], #>       "default": "user" #>     } #>   }, #>   "additionalProperties": true, #>   "required": [ #>     "name", #>     "age" #>   ] #> }

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: Open Data Contract Standard v3 (ODCS) industry format describing data products. typethis goes ways: typed models export ODCS YAML existing ODCS contracts import back model registry.

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: write_datacontract() file-IO convenience wrapper:
code: define_model("Order", fields = list(   order_id = field("character",                    primary_key = TRUE,                    validator   = string_pattern("^ORD-[0-9]+$")),   amount   = field("numeric",                    validator = numeric_range(0, 1e6),                    pii       = FALSE),   status   = field(t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped")),                    default = "new"),   customer = field("character",                    classification = "confidential",                    pii            = TRUE) ))  contract <- to_datacontract("Order",   info = list(name = "orders",               version = "1.0.0",               description = "Order records"))  str(contract, max.level = 2) #> List of 8 #>  $ apiVersion : chr "v3.0.2" #>  $ kind       : chr "DataContract" #>  $ id         : chr "Order" #>  $ status     : chr "draft" #>  $ name       : chr "orders" #>  $ version    : chr "1.0.0" #>  $ description:List of 1 #>   ..$ purpose: chr "Order records" #>  $ schema     :List of 1 #>   ..$ :List of 3 tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".yaml") write_datacontract("Order", tmp,   info = list(name = "orders", version = "1.0.0")) readLines(tmp, n = 6) #> [1] "apiVersion: v3.0.2" "kind: DataContract" "id: Order"          #> [4] "status: draft"      "name: orders"       "version: 1.0.0"

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: from_datacontract() reads contract back, calls define_model() every entry, assigns generated new_*() update_*() constructors envir.
code: env <- new.env() from_datacontract(tmp, envir = env) ls(env) #> [1] "new_Order"    "update_Order"  env$new_Order(order_id = "ORD-1", amount = 42, customer = "Ada") #> <Typed Model: Order> #> Fields: #>   order_id: character = ORD-1 #>   amount: numeric = 42 #>   customer: character = Ada #>   status: character = new

code: if (datacontract_cli_available()) {   datacontract_lint(tmp)   datacontract_test(tmp, server = "production")   datacontract_export(tmp, format = "jsonschema") }

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: OpenAPI 3.1 JSON-Schema-Draft-2020-12-compatible, JSON Schema machinery powers OpenAPI bridge well. Models become entries components.schemas; $ref strings rewritten #/$defs/X #/components/schemas/X. Typed functions become paths entry whose requestBody carries arguments whose 200 response carries return type.

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
code: define_model("Address2", fields = list(   street = field("character"),   city   = field("character") )) define_model("PersonDoc", fields = list(   id      = field("integer", primary_key = TRUE,                   validator = numeric_range(min = 1L)),   name    = field("character"),   address = field("Address2") ))  doc <- to_openapi(list("PersonDoc", "Address2"),   info = list(title = "People API", version = "1.0.0")) names(doc$components$schemas) #> [1] "PersonDoc" "Address2"

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
code: greet <- typed_function(   function(name, greeting = "Hi") paste(greeting, name),   arg_specs   = list(name = "character", greeting = "character"),   return_spec = "character" ) attr(greet, "openapi_op_id") <- "greet"  doc <- to_openapi(list("PersonDoc", greet),   info = list(title = "People API", version = "1.0.0")) names(doc$paths) #> [1] "/greet"

title: Interop: JSON Schema, ODCS, and OpenAPI
text: write_openapi() infers format file extension. Pass format = "json" (use .json path) JSON output.
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".yaml") write_openapi("PersonDoc", tmp,   info = list(title = "People API", version = "1.0.0"))  env <- new.env() from_openapi(tmp, envir = env) ls(env) #> [1] "new_Address2"     "new_PersonDoc"    "update_Address2"  "update_PersonDoc"

title: Composable type specifications
text: Use is_type_spec() detect composite spec runtime.

title: Composable type specifications
code: id <- t_union("integer", "character") is_type(1L,    id) #> [1] TRUE is_type("u-1", id) #> [1] TRUE is_type(1.5,   id) #> [1] FALSE

title: Composable type specifications
code: maybe_int <- t_nullable("integer") is_type(NULL, maybe_int) #> [1] TRUE is_type(1L,   maybe_int) #> [1] TRUE is_type("hi", maybe_int) #> [1] FALSE

title: Composable type specifications
text: t_list_of() accepts element type spec, including composites:
code: tags <- t_list_of("character", min_length = 1L) is_type(list("alpha", "beta"), tags) #> [1] TRUE is_type(list(),                tags) #> [1] FALSE  triple <- t_vector_of("integer", exact_length = 3L) is_type(1:3, triple) #> [1] TRUE is_type(1:5, triple) #> [1] FALSE mixed <- t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) is_type(list(1L, "two", 3L), mixed) #> [1] TRUE

title: Composable type specifications
text: t_enum() type-spec sibling enum_validator(). Use type-spec form want enum appear true type field() typed_function() surface JSON Schema typed enum keyword.
code: role <- t_enum(c("admin", "user", "guest")) is_type("admin", role) #> [1] TRUE is_type("root",  role) #> [1] FALSE

title: Composable type specifications
text: References resolve validation time, makes forward references (cycles) possible:
code: define_model("Address", fields = list(zip = field("character"))) addr <- t_model("Address") is_type(new_Address(zip = "10115"), addr) #> [1] TRUE

title: Composable type specifications
text: t_predicate() thin wrapper around custom predicate carries description. description surfaces error messages JSON Schema output.
code: positive <- t_predicate(   function(x) is.numeric(x) && all(x > 0),   description = "positive number" ) is_type(5,  positive) #> [1] TRUE is_type(-1, positive) #> [1] FALSE

what: Using specs in models and functions
title: Composable type specifications
text: Anywhere type = arg_specs = accepts builtin name, also accepts spec.
code: define_model("Order", fields = list(   id     = field(t_union("integer", "character")),   status = field(t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped")), default = "new"),   tags   = field(t_list_of("character"), default = list()) ))  ord <- new_Order(id = "ord-1", tags = list("priority", "rush")) ord$status #> [1] "new" ord$tags #> [[1]] #> [1] "priority" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "rush" greet <- typed_function(   function(name, n = 1L) paste(rep(name, n), collapse = " "),   arg_specs = list(     name = "character",     n    = t_union("integer", "numeric")   ),   return_spec = "character" ) greet("hi", n = 3) #> [1] "hi hi hi"

title: Composable type specifications
text: coerce_type() understands subset specs coercion clear meaning: t_nullable(...) — NULL passes ; otherwise inner spec drives coercion; t_union(...) — alternative tried order; first coerces validates wins; t_enum(...) — values already allowed set pass ; otherwise value coerced enum’s value type re-checked. kinds (t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_model(), t_predicate()) raise explicit error.
code: coerce_type("paid", t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped"))) #> [1] "paid" coerce_type(NULL,   t_nullable("integer")) #> NULL coerce_type("123",  t_union("integer", "character")) #> [1] 123

title: Validators and models
text: validator just function function(value) -> logical. package ships factories common rules; results carry constraint attribute bridges (JSON Schema, ODCS, OpenAPI) can emit native keywords (minimum, maxLength, pattern, …) instead opaque predicate stubs.

title: Validators and models
code: age <- numeric_range(0, 120) age(25) #> [1] TRUE age(150) #> [1] FALSE  # Probability strictly in (0, 1) prob <- numeric_range(0, 1, exclusive_min = TRUE, exclusive_max = TRUE) prob(0.5) #> [1] TRUE prob(0) #> [1] FALSE

title: Validators and models
code: name <- string_length(min_length = 1, max_length = 50) name("Ada Lovelace") #> [1] TRUE name("") #> [1] FALSE  email <- string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$") email("user@example.com") #> [1] TRUE email("not-an-email") #> [1] FALSE

title: Validators and models
code: pair <- vector_length(exact_len = 2) pair(c(1, 2)) #> [1] TRUE pair(c(1, 2, 3)) #> [1] FALSE  nums <- list_of("numeric", min_length = 1) nums(list(1, 2, 3)) #> [1] TRUE nums(list("a")) #> [1] FALSE  # Optional values: wrap any validator with `nullable()` optional_num <- nullable(function(x) is.numeric(x)) optional_num(NULL) #> [1] TRUE optional_num(5) #> [1] TRUE

title: Validators and models
code: is_orders <- dataframe_spec(   required_cols = c("id", "amount"),   min_rows = 1 ) is_orders(data.frame(id = 1:3, amount = c(10, 20, 30))) #> [1] TRUE is_orders(data.frame(id = integer())) #> [1] FALSE is_orders(data.frame(name = "Ada")) #> [1] FALSE

title: Validators and models
text: equivalent doubles type spec — usable inside field() typed_function() — see t_enum().
code: status <- enum_validator(c("active", "inactive", "pending")) status("active") #> [1] TRUE status("deleted") #> [1] FALSE

title: Validators and models
code: positive_num <- combine_validators(   function(x) is.numeric(x),   function(x) all(x > 0) ) positive_num(5) #> [1] TRUE positive_num(-1) #> [1] FALSE  num_or_str <- combine_validators(   function(x) is.numeric(x),   function(x) is.character(x),   all_of = FALSE ) num_or_str(5) #> [1] TRUE num_or_str("hi") #> [1] TRUE num_or_str(TRUE) #> [1] FALSE

title: Validators and models
text: validator_constraint() exposes structured constraint attached built-factories — useful tooling bridge functions:
code: validator_constraint(numeric_range(0, 10)) #> $kind #> [1] "numeric_range" #>  #> $min #> [1] 0 #>  #> $max #> [1] 10 #>  #> $exclusive_min #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $exclusive_max #> [1] FALSE validator_constraint(function(x) x > 0)  # NULL for plain user predicates #> NULL

title: Validators and models
text: Use define_model() describe record. creates new_<Class>() constructor update_<Class>() helper calling environment.
code: define_model("Person", fields = list(   name = field("character"),   age  = field("integer", validator = numeric_range(0, 120)),   email = field("character",                 validator = string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+$")) ))  p <- new_Person(name = "Ada", age = 36L, email = "ada@example.com") p #> <Typed Model: Person> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada #>   age: integer = 36 #>   email: character = ada@example.com

title: Validators and models
code: define_model("Config", fields = list(   host  = field("character", default = "localhost"),   port  = field("integer",   default = 8080L),   debug = field("logical",   default = FALSE) ))  new_Config()$host #> [1] "localhost" new_Config(host = "api.example.com", port = 443L)$port #> [1] 443

title: Validators and models
text: field nullable = TRUE allowed NULL (omitted construction without default). Without , omission error unless default set.
code: define_model("Profile", fields = list(   name = field("character"),   bio  = field("character", nullable = TRUE) ))  new_Profile(name = "Ada")$bio #> NULL new_Profile(name = "Ada", bio = "Mathematician")$bio #> [1] "Mathematician"

title: Validators and models
text: .strict = TRUE rejects unknown fields construction:
code: define_model("StrictPoint", fields = list(   x = field("numeric"),   y = field("numeric") ), .strict = TRUE)  new_StrictPoint(x = 1, y = 2, z = 3) #> Error: #> ! Extra fields not allowed for StrictPoint: z

title: Validators and models
text: field’s type may registered model class name. constructor check value instance class. Use t_model("Address") want type spec form can combined t_nullable() t_list_of().
code: define_model("Address", fields = list(   street = field("character"),   city   = field("character") ))  define_model("Person2", fields = list(   name    = field("character"),   address = field("Address") ))  new_Person2(   name    = "Ada",   address = new_Address(street = "Newstead", city = "Nottingham") ) #> <Typed Model: Person2> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada #>   address: Address = <Address of length 2>

title: Validators and models
text: field() accepts validator callback runs type check:
code: define_model("Coupon", fields = list(   code     = field("character",                    validator = string_length(min_length = 4, max_length = 12)),   discount = field("numeric",                    validator = numeric_range(0, 1, exclusive_max = TRUE)) ))  new_Coupon(code = "SAVE20", discount = 0.2) #> <Typed Model: Coupon> #> Fields: #>   code: character = SAVE20 #>   discount: numeric = 0.2 new_Coupon(code = "AB", discount = 0.2) #> Error: #> ! Validation failed for field 'code' in Coupon

title: Validators and models
text: update_<Class>() preferred way mutate — preserves S3 class revalidates: mutation across models without class-specific updater, use update_model().
code: schema_keys <- names(get_schema(p)) schema_keys #> [1] "name"  "age"   "email"  is_model(p) #> [1] TRUE model_to_list(p) #> <Typed Model: Person> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada #>   age: integer = 36 #>   email: character = ada@example.com  result <- validate_model(p) result$valid #> [1] TRUE p2 <- update_Person(p, age = 37L) p2$age #> [1] 37

title: Validators and models
text: field() accepts metadata travels to_json_schema(), to_datacontract(), to_openapi() exporters effect runtime validation. See interop vignette key .
code: field(   "character",   primary_key    = TRUE,   pii            = TRUE,   classification = "confidential",   tags           = c("audit", "lifecycle"),   examples       = list("ORD-1", "ORD-2") ) #> $type #> [1] "character" #>  #> $default #> NULL #>  #> $validator #> NULL #>  #> $nullable #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $description #> [1] "" #>  #> $primary_key #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $unique #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $pii #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $classification #> [1] "confidential" #>  #> $tags #> [1] "audit"     "lifecycle" #>  #> $examples #> $examples[[1]] #> [1] "ORD-1" #>  #> $examples[[2]] #> [1] "ORD-2" #>  #>  #> $references #> NULL #>  #> $quality #> NULL

title: Validators and models
text: Validation per-call cost. hot paths: pass .validate = FALSE define_model() disable checks construction; pass validate = FALSE typed_function() effect function calls; check upstream trust boundaries. can still call validate_model() demand re-check instance series unchecked updates.

text: Runtime type safety validation R — inspired Python’s pydantic mypy.  https://fabiandistler.github.io/typethis/ typethis lets describe data functions expect, checks runtime — clearly, helpful error messages, without leaving plain R.
code: define_model("User", fields = list(   name  = field("character"),   age   = field("integer", validator = numeric_range(0, 120)),   email = field("character",                 validator = string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$")) ))  new_User(name = "Ada", age = 36L, email = "ada@example.com") #> <Typed Model: User> #> Fields: #>   name: character = Ada #>   age: integer = 36 #>   email: character = ada@example.com  new_User(name = "Ada", age = 200L, email = "ada@example.com") #> Error: Validation failed for field 'age' in User

title: Type Safety and Validation for R

what: What you can do with it
title: Type Safety and Validation for R
text: Check types runtime. is_type(), assert_type(), validate_type(), coerce_type() everyday cases. Wrap functions type checks. typed_function() validates arguments every call return value every exit. Describe data typed models. define_model("Class", fields = list(…)) generates new_Class() update_Class() constructors field-level validation, defaults, nullability. Compose richer types. t_union(), t_nullable(), t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_enum(), t_model(), t_predicate() build specs work everywhere type name . Reach ecosystems. Export import JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12), Open Data Contract Standard v3, OpenAPI 3.1 without leaving R.

title: Type Safety and Validation for R
code: library(typethis)  # 1. Type checks is_type(1:3, "integer")            # TRUE assert_type(42, "character", "x")  # Error: 'x' must be character, got integer  # 2. Typed functions greet <- typed_function(   function(name, times = 1L) paste(rep(name, times), collapse = " "),   arg_specs = list(name = "character", times = "integer"),   return_spec = "character" ) greet("hi", times = 3L)            # "hi hi hi" greet("hi", times = "3")           # Error: 'times' must be integer, got character  # 3. Typed models define_model("Point", fields = list(   x = field("numeric"),   y = field("numeric") )) p <- new_Point(x = 1, y = 2) update_Point(p, x = 5)$x           # 5  # 4. Composable specs status <- t_enum(c("new", "paid", "shipped")) is_type("paid", status)            # TRUE is_type("done", status)            # FALSE

what: Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS v3) bridge — datacontract
title: Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS v3) bridge — datacontract
text: Convert typed models Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS) v3 YAML format, run datacontract CLI R. Pydantic kind bridge: datacontract export --format pydantic-model generates Pydantic source code contract. typethis closes loop R: typed models can exported ODCS YAML, existing ODCS contracts can loaded back model registry runtime new_*() / update_*() constructors. Constructs without native ODCS representation (data frames, factors, unions, custom predicate functions) emitted x-typethis-* extension keys bridge round-trips typethis-aware tooling. Key entry points: to_datacontract() / write_datacontract() — export. read_datacontract() / from_datacontract() — import. datacontract_lint() / datacontract_test() / datacontract_export() — thin wrappers around upstream CLI.

what: Define a typed data model — define_model
title: Define a typed data model — define_model
text: Defines model class — record type named fields, types, optional validators, defaults, nullability — creates two helpers calling environment: new_<ClassName>() — constructor. Validates argument field spec applies defaults. update_<ClassName>(instance, …) — returns copy named fields replaced revalidated. Define field field(); pass named list fields. Fields can use type accepted elsewhere typethis (builtin character names, predicate functions, registered model class names, composite type_spec objects).

title: Define a typed data model — define_model
code: define_model(..., fields = NULL, .validate = TRUE, .strict = FALSE)

title: Define a typed data model — define_model
text: ... class name first positional argument (single character scalar). Field definitions can also passed name = field(...) arguments instead via fields. fields Named list field definitions built field() (bare type names). .validate FALSE, validation skipped construction (useful hot paths). .strict TRUE, constructor rejects unknown fields.

title: Define a typed data model — define_model
text: Invisibly NULL. constructor updater assigned calling environment.

title: Define a typed data model — define_model
code: define_model("User", fields = list(   name  = field("character"),   age   = field("integer", validator = numeric_range(0, 120),                 default = 0L),   email = field("character",                 validator = string_pattern("^[^@]+@[^@]+$")) ))  u <- new_User(name = "Ada", email = "ada@example.com") u$age   # default applied #> [1] 0  u2 <- update_User(u, age = 36L) u2$age #> [1] 36  # Strict mode rejects unknown fields define_model("StrictPoint", fields = list(   x = field("numeric"),   y = field("numeric") ), .strict = TRUE)  tryCatch(   new_StrictPoint(x = 1, y = 2, z = 3),   error = function(e) conditionMessage(e) ) #> [1] "Extra fields not allowed for StrictPoint: z"

title: Define a model field — field
text: Builds field definition use inside define_model(). field carries type, optional default, optional validator, optional nullability — plus number metadata slots (primary_key, pii, tags, ...) travel to_datacontract() / to_openapi() / to_json_schema() bridges effect runtime validation.

title: Define a model field — field
text: type Type specification. default Default value field omitted construction. validator Optional value-level validator function. nullable TRUE, field accepts NULL. description Free-text description; surfaces JSON Schema ODCS export. primary_key Logical. ODCS metadata . unique Logical. ODCS metadata . pii Logical. ODCS metadata . classification Optional classification label ("public", "internal", "confidential", ...). ODCS metadata . tags Optional character vector free-form tags. examples Optional list/vector example values. references Optional named list list(model = "Order", field = "id") describing foreign-key style reference. ODCS metadata . quality Optional list quality checks (named list type, description, engine-specific keys like query).

title: Import an ODCS contract into the typethis model registry — from_datacontract
code: if (requireNamespace("yaml", quietly = TRUE)) {   define_model("Order", fields = list(     order_id = field("character", primary_key = TRUE),     amount   = field("numeric")   ))    tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".yaml")   write_datacontract("Order", tmp,     info = list(name = "orders", version = "1.0.0"))    env <- new.env()   from_datacontract(tmp, envir = env)   ls(env)  # new_Order, update_Order } #> [1] "new_Order"    "update_Order"

title: JSON Schema export — json_schema
text: Convert typed models, type specs, validators, field definitions JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) fragments. result R list ready jsonlite::toJSON(..., auto_unbox = TRUE). Builtin validator factories (numeric_range(), string_length(), string_pattern(), vector_length(), enum_validator()) attach constraint attribute exporter reads — range pattern constraints surface native minimum / maxLength / pattern keys rather opaque predicate stubs. title: Composable type specifications — type_spec
text: Structured, composable type specifications. work everywhere type name — is_type(), assert_type(), validate_type(), field(), typed_function(), to_json_schema() — compose: t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) valid.

title: Composable type specifications — type_spec
text: constructors return objects class type_spec. Plain character strings (e.g. "numeric") predicate functions continue work type arguments without change; internally normalized type_spec objects. Available constructors: t_union() — match several alternatives. t_nullable() — also accept NULL. t_list_of() — list elements given type, optional length. t_vector_of() — atomic vector given builtin type, length. t_enum() — value fixed set. t_model() — instance registered typed model. t_predicate() — wrap predicate function description. Use is_type_spec() detect composite spec runtime.

what: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
text: Returns wrapped version fn validates argument spec arg_specs every call, (optionally) return value return_spec. Calls violate spec raise informative error — just — fn runs. Argument specs may character builtins, predicate functions, type_spec (e.g. t_union(), t_list_of()). Coercion can enabled per call via coerce = TRUE. R calling conventions supported: positional, named, reordered named, mixed, ... passthrough.

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
code: typed_function(   fn,   arg_specs = NULL,   return_spec = NULL,   validate = TRUE,   coerce = FALSE,   arg_types = NULL,   return_type = NULL )

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
text: fn underlying function. arg_specs Named list (character vector) argument specifications. Names must match argument names fn. return_spec Specification return value, NULL skip. validate FALSE, type checks skipped (useful hot paths). coerce TRUE, arguments match first run coerce_type() assertion. arg_types, return_type Deprecated aliases arg_specs / return_spec. New code use latter.

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
text: function formals fn. Carries arg_specs, return_spec, typed = TRUE attributes.

title: Wrap a function with input/output type checks — typed_function
code: add <- typed_function(   function(x, y) x + y,   arg_specs = c(x = "numeric", y = "numeric"),   return_spec = "numeric" ) add(2, 3) #> [1] 5 add(x = 2, y = 3) #> [1] 5 add(y = 3, x = 2) #> [1] 5  # Argument violation err <- tryCatch(add("a", "b"), error = function(e) conditionMessage(e)) err #> [1] "Type error: 'x' must be numeric, got character"  # ... passthrough total <- typed_function(   function(x, ...) sum(x, ...),   arg_specs = c(x = "numeric") ) total(c(1, NA, 3), na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 4  # Coercion add_lenient <- typed_function(   function(x, y) x + y,   arg_specs = c(x = "numeric", y = "numeric"),   coerce = TRUE ) add_lenient("5", "3") #> [1] 8

text: exported API grouped following families. help page function lists family "See also" section, know one function can navigate neighbours. Type specifications t_union(), t_nullable(), t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_enum(), t_model(), t_predicate(), is_type_spec() — composable specs work everywhere type name . Validators numeric_range(), string_length(), string_pattern(), vector_length(), dataframe_spec(), enum_validator(), list_of(), nullable(), combine_validators(), validator_constraint() — value-level rules attached fields arguments. Typed functions typed_function(), signature(), with_signature(), is_typed(), get_signature(), typed_method(), validate_call() — wrap function call validated. Typed models define_model(), field(), is_model(), get_schema(), validate_model(), update_model(), model_to_list() — describe record type field-level validation, defaults, nullability. JSON Schema export to_json_schema() — emit JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) fragments typed models, type specs, validators. Data Contract bridge to_datacontract(), write_datacontract(), read_datacontract(), from_datacontract(), datacontract_lint(), datacontract_test(), datacontract_export(), datacontract_cli_available() — round-trip Open Data Contract Standard v3. title: Validate a call to a typed function without executing it — validate_call
code: f <- typed_function(   function(x, y) x + y,   arg_specs = c(x = "numeric", y = "numeric") ) validate_call(f, x = 5, y = 3) #> $valid #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $errors #> NULL #>  validate_call(f, x = "a", y = 3) #> $valid #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $errors #> [1] "Type error: 'x' must be numeric, got character" #>

title: Read the constraint descriptor attached to a validator — validator_constraint

title: Read the constraint descriptor attached to a validator — validator_constraint
code: validator_constraint(numeric_range(0, 10)) #> $kind #> [1] "numeric_range" #>  #> $min #> [1] 0 #>  #> $max #> [1] 10 #>  #> $exclusive_min #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $exclusive_max #> [1] FALSE #>  validator_constraint(string_length(max_length = 50)) #> $kind #> [1] "string_length" #>  #> $min_length #> [1] 0 #>  #> $max_length #> [1] 50 #>  validator_constraint(function(x) x > 0) #> NULL

what: Validate vector or list length — vector_length

title: Validate vector or list length — vector_length
text: min_len Minimum length (inclusive). max_len Maximum length (inclusive, defaults Inf). exact_len non-NULL, value must exactly length.

title: Validate vector or list length — vector_length
text: validator function function(value) -> logical.

title: Validate vector or list length — vector_length
code: pair <- vector_length(exact_len = 2) pair(c(1, 2)) #> [1] TRUE pair(c(1, 2, 3)) #> [1] FALSE  nonempty <- vector_length(min_len = 1) nonempty(integer()) #> [1] FALSE nonempty(1:5) #> [1] TRUE

title: typethis (development version)
text: to_openapi() (S3 generic) converts typed models, model constructors, instances, typed functions, mixed lists OpenAPI 3.1 document fragment. Schemas land components.schemas; $ref strings rewritten JSON Schema’s #/$defs/X form OpenAPI’s #/components/schemas/X form. Typed functions become paths entry JSON requestBody (arg_specs entry property; arguments without defaults end required) 200 response derived return_spec. from_openapi() reads OpenAPI 3.x document (path, URL, parsed list) registers entry components.schemas typed model generated new_*() / update_*() constructors. $refs components.schemas resolve t_model() references; inline object properties registered typed models. write_openapi() / read_openapi() wrap file IO. Format inferred file extension (.yaml/.yml → YAML via yaml, .json → JSON via jsonlite); pass format = "yaml" / "json" override. bridge sits top to_json_schema() (v0.3) — composite type specs validator constraints flow unchanged, new mapping code needed.

title: typethis (development version)
text: jsonlite now also used (addition JSON Schema export) write_openapi(format = "json") / read_openapi() .json paths. Still Suggests.

title: typethis (development version)
text: coerce_type() now accepts type_spec argument composable cases coercion clear meaning: t_nullable() (NULL passes , otherwise inner spec drives coercion), t_union() (alternative tried order; first coerces validates wins), t_enum() (value accepted directly already allowed set, coerced enum’s value type re-checked). type_spec kinds (t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_model(), t_predicate()) still error, clearer message naming unsupported kind. README vignette updated cover v0.3 (composable type specs, JSON Schema export) v0.4 (Data Contract bridge).

title: typethis (development version)
text: New test nested-object registration from_datacontract() (covers implicit define_model_in() side-effect nested ODCS object properties). New .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml runs R CMD check (errors warnings) macOS, Windows, Ubuntu (release, devel, oldrel-1) plus lintr job every push PR.

title: typethis (development version)
text: to_datacontract() (S3 generic) converts typed models, model constructors, instances Open Data Contract Standard v3.x contracts native R lists, ready yaml::write_yaml(). write_datacontract() writes contract directly .yaml file. from_datacontract() reads contract YAML (path, URL, parsed list) registers schema entry typed model generated new_*() / update_*() constructors. read_datacontract() parses contract YAML without registering anything. Thin wrappers around datacontract CLI: datacontract_lint(), datacontract_test(), datacontract_export(), plus datacontract_cli_available() capability detection. field() gained ODCS metadata arguments: primary_key, unique, pii, classification, tags, examples, references, quality. round-trip contract bridge introspectable, effect runtime validation. Validator constraints (numeric_range, string_length, string_pattern, vector_length, enum_validator, dataframe_spec) emitted native ODCS constraint fields (minimum, maxLength, pattern, enum, …). Constructs without native ODCS mapping (data frames, factors, unions, custom predicate functions) emit x-typethis-* extension keys bridge round-trips typethis-aware tooling.

title: typethis (development version)
text: yaml (>= 2.3.0) added Suggests. Required write_datacontract(), read_datacontract(), from_datacontract().

title: typethis (development version)
text: New type_spec S3 class structured, composable type specifications. New constructors: t_union(), t_nullable(), t_list_of(), t_vector_of(), t_enum(), t_model(), t_predicate(). compose: t_list_of(t_union("integer", "character")) valid. composite specs work is_type(), assert_type(), typed_function() (arg_specs/return_spec), field(). Backward compatible: plain character builtin names predicate functions continue work everywhere . Helper is_type_spec() detect composite specs.

title: typethis (development version)
text: New to_json_schema() (S3 generic) serializes typed models, type specs, validator closures, field() definitions JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) fragments suitable jsonlite::toJSON(). Methods: to_json_schema.default(), to_json_schema.type_spec(), to_json_schema.typed_model(). Builtin validator factories (numeric_range, string_length, string_pattern, vector_length, enum_validator, list_of, dataframe_spec, nullable, combine_validators) now attach structured constraint attribute returned closure. Use validator_constraint() read . closure otherwise unchanged remains callable . Nested models become $ref entries auto-populated $defs; cyclic references terminate via stub--fill protocol. Constructs without canonical JSON Schema mapping (data frames, factors, custom predicates) emit x-typethis-* extension keys.
